steps.title: Apply the vertical shift
steps.content:
If the graph shown is $y = f(x) + 2$, then $f(x) = y - 2$ — shift the displayed graph down by 2.

The displayed equation has the form $-2^x + k$ where $k$ is the horizontal asymptote. The graph's asymptote is at $y = 5$, so the displayed function is $-2^x + 5$.
